tier_legend.1A: Authoritative — first-party disclosure (earnings, filing, press release, sworn affidavit)
tier_legend.2A: Derived — deterministic calc from Tier 1 inputs, formula documented
tier_legend.2B: Triangulated — squeezed by multiple Tier 1 anchors via methodology
tier_legend.3A: Anchored projection — trend extrapolation from a Tier 1/2 base year using a cited growth rate
tier_legend.3B: Interpolated projection — curve fit between two Tier 1 anchor years
tier_legend.3C: Scenario assumption — hand-picked rate / ratio (must be flagged)
tier_legend.4: Editorial — round-number guess, no lineage. Replace ASAP.

_schema.notes: Per-cell tier/src tags reflect the framework in model-assumptions.md §6 + the conversation on 2026-04-11. costParams are per-year so cost-rate constants live with the data, not the renderer. vcSubsidy (when present) overrides the rate-based VC bypass calc.

infrastructureCapex.src: Mag 7 AI capex ~$250B — combined AI-related capital expenditure from Apple, Microsoft, Google, Amazon, Meta, NVIDIA, Tesla. Earnings-call aggregation (AI Ledger Data Vault, DP-017).

totalCapex.src: Full industry AI CapEx $380B — Mag 7 ($250B) + neoclouds, China, sovereign, Oracle, enterprise. Aligned with Follow the CapEx page. NVIDIA DC revenue ($194B) ≈ 55% of total.

frontierFunding.src: Cumulative committed funding to frontier AI labs through end-2025. OpenAI ~$60B (pre-Mar 2026 round), Anthropic ~$22B (pre-Series G), xAI ~$20B (Sacra), plus AI-native startups via PitchBook ~$150B total VC into AI 2025 less hyperscaler capex overlap (AI Ledger DP-019).

totalCustomerRevenue.src: AI Ledger rollup from entity filings

totalVCSubsidy.src: AI Ledger methodology §7.2 reports $9.80B; routing-derived $9.39B (4% rounding from per-channel allocation approximations)

buyers.label: Consumer
buyers.src: AI Ledger rollup; consumer split per The Information

buyers.label: SME
buyers.src: AI Ledger rollup from entity filings

buyers.label: Enterprise
buyers.src: OpenAI press release Mar 2026; >40% enterprise share corroborated by The Information

buyers.label: VC/Investors
buyers.src: Sum of published 2025 operating losses (OpenAI ~$6B, Anthropic ~$3B, Google ~$0.5B). Routing rounds to 9.39.

channels.label: Model Subs
channels.src: AI Ledger rollup from entity filings

channels.label: Model API
channels.src: AI Ledger rollup from entity filings

channels.label: Hyperscalers
channels.src: AI Ledger rollup (Azure OpenAI + Bedrock + Vertex resold)

channels.label: AI Native Apps
channels.src: AI Ledger rollup (Cursor, Glean, Harvey, Perplexity etc.)

channels.label: IaaS & Agg.
channels.src: AI Ledger rollup (Together, Fireworks, Groq, OpenRouter aggregated)

channels.label: Trad. SaaS
channels.src: AI Ledger rollup (MSFT/CRM/NOW AI SKU revenue per state-of-AI SKU analysis)

providers.label: OpenAI
providers.src: wq-055 engine — customer_revenue=$9.3147B (wq-048 engine); inference_cost=$5B (sourced); opex=$8.65B (derived); vc_subsidy=$4.3353B (balancing_plug_derived)

providers.label: Anthropic
providers.src: wq-055 engine — customer_revenue=$4.71B (wq-048 engine); inference_cost=$2.5905B (estimated); opex=$4.71B (estimated); vc_subsidy=$2.5905B (balancing_plug_derived)

providers.label: Google/Gemini
providers.src: wq-055 engine — customer_revenue=$2.2516B (wq-048 engine); inference_cost=$0.6755B (estimated); opex=$2.2516B (estimated); vc_subsidy=$0.6755B (balancing_plug_derived)

providers.label: IaaS/Open
providers.src: wq-055 engine — customer_revenue=$0.5B (wq-048 engine); inference_cost=$0.4B (preserved_hand_curated); opex=$0.4B (preserved_hand_curated); vc_subsidy=$0.3B (preserved_hand_curated)

providers.label: Other Model Providers
providers.src: wq-055 small-provider aggregation: xai, mistral, deepseek, minimax, moonshot (value < 2.0% of total AND outside top 4)

outcomes.label: Inference
outcomes.src: = sum(provider × inferencePct 0.57). Cross-checks AI Ledger rollup $14.03B.

outcomes.label: Other Operating Costs
outcomes.src: = sum(provider × (1 − inferencePct)). AI Ledger rollup has $11.08B; 0.48 gap from routing approximations.

outcomes.label: Generated Cashflow
outcomes.src: = channel margins (Hyperscaler 0.20×$2.15B + Trad SaaS 0.60×$2.94B). Cross-checks AI Ledger rollup $2.15B.

marginPcts.src: Industry standard hyperscaler take rate (~20%) and SaaS margin (~60%)

aiNativeIaasShare.src: Tuned so IaaS/Open routing balances at $0.8B (AI Ledger methodology §7.2)

providerRates.src: Set to ensure all providers cap at costPct=1.0 (loss-making in 2025 per AI Ledger methodology). Distribution irrelevant when vcSubsidy is explicit.

vcSubsidy.src: Per-provider routing deficit using AI Ledger provider values + chPass routing. Sum 9.39B vs AI Ledger methodology §7.2 9.80B (4% rounding).

infrastructureCapex.src: Mag 7 + AI infra capex ~$400B (Goldman Jan 2026 projects $500B+; Meta alone $125B authoritative per AI Ledger DP-040; MSFT ~$100B, Google nearly doubling per TrendForce, AMZN ~$80B). Conservative midpoint.

totalCapex.src: Full industry AI CapEx $550B — Mag 7 ($400B) + neoclouds, China, sovereign, enterprise. Aligned with Follow the CapEx page.

frontierFunding.src: Cumulative committed funding through end-2026. OpenAI $122B confirmed (Mar 2026 press release; AI Ledger entity database openai.2024.total_funding, Authoritative). Anthropic $52B (current $30B Series G + ~$22B prior). xAI $35B (Sacra post-SpaceX merger reporting). Plus AI-native VC.

2026E._scenario: Realistic — provider values and VC subsidies anchored to published guidance and operating losses. Previously an 'optimistic breakeven' scenario with $1.1B VC; now aligned with AI Ledger entity database 2026.operating_loss projections.

totalCustomerRevenue.src: Sum of buyers. Providers now anchored Tier 3A (OpenAI/Anthropic) with VC explicit (Tier 3A).

totalVCSubsidy.src: Sum of explicit vcSubsidy per provider, anchored to AI Ledger entity database operating_loss projections (OpenAI $14B, Anthropic ~$8B implied by burn trajectory to 9% by 2027).

buyers.label: Consumer
buyers.src: Scenario: ChatGPT 50M paid subs × $20/mo × 12 ≈ $12B (OpenAI press release Mar 2026, Authoritative) + Gemini Advanced + Claude Pro + Perplexity Pro + X Premium. Anchor = 50M paid subscriber count.

buyers.label: SME
buyers.src: Scenario: Cursor $2B ARR (AI Ledger source scan 2026-04-09, Corroborated) + Claude Code $2.5B ARR (AI Ledger DP-058, Corroborated) + small-business tier of major apps. Majority SME usage.

buyers.label: Enterprise
buyers.src: Scenario: $35B core AI spend (Ramp $530K avg AI contract × ~60K paying enterprises, AI Ledger source scan 2026-04-03) + $2.5B advertising spend on OpenAI's ad pilot (Axios / AI Ledger source scan 2026-04-11). OpenAI Mar 2026 press: >40% enterprise share (Authoritative). Companies, not consumers, fund ad inventory.

buyers.label: VC/Investors
buyers.src: Sum of per-provider vcSubsidy. Anchored to AI Ledger entity database operating_loss: OpenAI 2026.operating_loss = $14B (medium confidence). Anthropic derived: $7.9B loss implied by 9% burn-rate target by 2027 applied to ramp. Google/Other small losses from continuing investment. Previously optimistic at $1.1B — now realigned.

channels.label: Ads
channels.src: Funded entirely by Enterprise advertisers (companies, not consumers, buy ad inventory). Routes 100% to OpenAI as sole frontier ad-monetiser (Axios / AI Ledger source scan 2026-04-11).

channels.label: Model Subs
channels.src: Scenario: ChatGPT Plus/Pro/Team + Claude Pro/Max + Gemini Advanced + Perplexity Pro + Copilot Pro. Anchored to 50M+ subscriber base (OpenAI press release).

channels.label: Model API
channels.src: Scenario: direct API revenue (OpenAI API + Anthropic API + Gemini API direct). Anchors: OpenAI Mar 2026 $2B/month authoritative; API ~30% of OpenAI per 2025 revenue_by_channel.

channels.label: Hyperscalers
channels.src: Scenario: Azure OpenAI + Bedrock (Anthropic) + Vertex (Gemini) resold, including ~20% hyperscaler margin. Scaled up from $2.15B 2025 baseline (~4.7x).

channels.label: AI Native Apps
channels.src: Scenario: Cursor + Claude Code (partial) + Glean + Harvey + Perplexity + Clay + other AI-native SaaS. Scaled from $3.36B 2025 baseline.

channels.label: IaaS & Agg.
channels.src: Scenario: Together + Fireworks + Groq + OpenRouter + direct open-model APIs (DeepSeek, Moonshot, MiniMax). Scaled from $0.3B 2025 baseline (~17x, reflecting explosive Chinese provider growth — OpenRouter signals confirm).

channels.label: Trad. SaaS
channels.src: Scenario: MSFT M365 Copilot + Salesforce Agentforce + ServiceNow AI + Google Workspace AI + Adobe Firefly. 60% margin retained by SaaS vendor per AI Ledger enterprise SaaS AI SKU analysis.

providers.label: OpenAI
providers.src: Anchored: customer revenue ~$29B (Mar 2026 $2B/month × 12 ramped Q4-exit) + VC subsidy $14B (AI Ledger entity database openai.2026.operating_loss, medium confidence) + ads $2.5B. Bumped from $25.1B in optimistic scenario to $43B to match published loss trajectory.

providers.label: Anthropic
providers.src: Anchored: customer revenue ~$15B (year-average between Feb 2026 $14B and Mar 2026 $19B ARR) + VC subsidy $7.9B (implied by 9% burn target by 2027 applied to 2026 ramp; 2025 operating_loss was $5.2B, 2026 higher during scale).

providers.label: Google (Gemini)
providers.src: Scenario: customer ~$4.3B + VC $2.2B. Scaled from 2025 ARR $4.2B with modest loss continuing during Gemini scale-up. No direct 2026 guidance anchor.

providers.label: IaaS/Open
providers.src: Scenario: open model adoption via AI Native Apps. Profitable aggregators (Together, Fireworks, Groq) + direct Chinese provider APIs. Breakeven/small profit, no VC subsidy.

providers.label: Other Model Providers
providers.src: Scenario bucket: xAI Grok ~$500M customer (Sacra; AI Ledger source scan 2026-04-11) + $0.8B VC subsidy (xAI burn continues). Plus Cohere + Mistral + smaller labs.

outcomes.label: Inference
outcomes.src: = sum(provider × INF 0.47). All loss-making except IaaS/Open.

outcomes.label: Generated Cashflow
outcomes.src: = channel margins ($7.7B) + IaaS/Open surplus ($0.8B); provider surplus collapses vs old scenario because OpenAI/Anthropic/Google no longer profitable.

inferencePct.src: Scenario: inference cost share drops from 57% (2025) to 47% as efficiency improves

aiNativeIaasShare.src: Scenario: 30% of AI Native App revenue routes to open models via IaaS aggregators

providerRates.src: Rates tuned to make each provider's implied costPct match its scenario VC subsidy. OpenAI cost/rev = 43/29 ≈ 1.48; Anthropic = 23/15.1 ≈ 1.52; Google = 6.5/4.3 ≈ 1.52; IaaS profitable at 0.89 costPct; Other = 3.1x customer rev (big xAI burn).

vcSubsidy.src: Explicit per-provider VC bypass, derived from routing-required deficit at target provider values. OpenAI $14B anchored to AI Ledger entity database openai.2026.operating_loss (Fortune). Anthropic $7.9B anchored to burn-rate target. Google/Other editorial within scenario.

infrastructureCapex.src: Scenario: ~$500B Mag 7 + AI-native infra capex. Stargate ramp ($100B/yr trajectory toward $500B over 4 years, scan-various), Meta + Google + MSFT continuing growth, plus sovereign/Chinese DC build-out. No 2027 anchor available yet; extrapolation from 2026E trajectory.

totalCapex.src: Full industry AI CapEx $700B — Mag 7 ($500B) + neoclouds, China, sovereign, enterprise. Aligned with Follow the CapEx page.

frontierFunding.src: Scenario: assumes one more major OpenAI round (~$28B) and Anthropic Series H (~$23B) to fund continued operating losses. xAI continued at SpaceX-merged trajectory. Editorial extrapolation; will update with actuals as rounds close.

2027E._scenario: Transition — 2027 shows frontier providers approaching sustainability but not yet at zero burn. VC subsidy declines 88% from 2026E but IaaS is only fully profitable player.

totalCustomerRevenue.src: Sum of buyers. OpenAI anchored to Fortune $280B 2030 trajectory (Tier 3A); other cells Tier 3C scenario.

totalVCSubsidy.src: Sum of per-provider vcSubsidy. Scenario: modest ongoing losses during transition to sustainability. Small compared to 2026E $25B, large vs AI Ledger entity database 2029.cash_burn $115B cumulative suggests 2027 still has meaningful burn.

buyers.label: Consumer
buyers.src: Scenario: 80-100M paid consumer subs across OpenAI/Anthropic/Google at blended $20/mo. Extrapolated from 2026E anchor.

buyers.label: SME
buyers.src: Scenario: $19.5B core SME AI spend (Cursor/Claude Code/Cline + AI SaaS) + $1.5B SME advertising on AI-native ad surfaces. SME share of ad spend grows as ads productise beyond enterprise.

buyers.label: Enterprise
buyers.src: Scenario: $63B core enterprise AI spend (Ramp trend extrapolated) + $5.5B enterprise advertising. Enterprise still ~79% of total ad spend, consistent with broader digital ad market mix.

buyers.label: VC/Investors
buyers.src: Sum of per-provider vcSubsidy: OpenAI $0.8B + Anthropic $0.7B + Google $0.3B + Other $1.4B. Transition year; not zero burn but big drop from 2026E $25B.

channels.label: Ads
channels.src: Funded by Enterprise ($5.5B, ~79%) + SME ($1.5B, ~21%) advertisers. Total interpolated from $2.5B (2026, Tier 3A) → $53B (2029, AI Ledger source scan 2026-04-11) on ~177% CAGR curve. Routes 100% to OpenAI.

channels.label: Model Subs
channels.src: Scenario: scaled from 2026E by ~47% (subscriber growth + price increases)

channels.label: Model API
channels.src: Scenario: scaled from 2026E; API volume grows but blended price/M falls (OpenRouter signals ongoing)

channels.label: Hyperscalers
channels.src: Scenario: Azure/Bedrock/Vertex + emerging hyperscaler AI SKUs

channels.label: AI Native Apps
channels.src: Scenario: AI-native SaaS continues dominating coding/search/knowledge use cases

channels.label: IaaS & Agg.
channels.src: Scenario: open model inference revenue doubles from 2026E; Chinese providers + Meta API launch assumed

channels.label: Trad. SaaS
channels.src: Scenario: MSFT/CRM/NOW AI SKU attach reaches ~30% of customer base

providers.label: OpenAI
providers.src: Anchored: Fortune (Feb 2026) leaked OpenAI 2030 revenue forecast $280B (AI Ledger DP-067; entity database openai.2030.arr); 2027 interpolation ≈ $50B exit ARR. Year-average collected + VC $0.8B + ads $7B ≈ $43B. Bumped from $41.1B to fix routing artifact (inflow was > value).

providers.label: Anthropic
providers.src: Scenario: extrapolated from 2026E $23B at ~26% YoY growth (decelerating as base compounds). 9% burn rate target implies $2.6B loss, but explicit VC here is lower as routing absorbs most.

providers.label: Google (Gemini)
providers.src: Scenario: continued share growth; Gemini 3.x adoption + Vertex enterprise. Near breakeven.

providers.label: IaaS/Open
providers.src: Scenario: open models continue gaining share via aggregators. Only provider fully profitable in 2027E (rate 0.40 < 0.53).

providers.label: Other Model Providers
providers.src: Scenario bucket: xAI + Chinese labs + emerging players. xAI burn dominates this cell's VC need.

outcomes.label: Generated Cashflow
outcomes.src: = channel margins ($14.2B) + IaaS surplus ($1.9B). Provider surplus still modest because only IaaS is profitable in this scenario.

providerRates.src: Scenario: industry transitions to sustainability. Loss-makers (OpenAI/Anthropic/Google) at costPct ~1.03; IaaS profitable at 0.87; xAI/Other still burning at 1.82x.

vcSubsidy.src: Explicit per-provider VC bypass, routing-derived given target provider values. Total $3.1B represents transition burn; not a direct anchor claim but consistent with 'approaching sustainability' narrative.
